Title: User registration and faculty assignment
Preconditions:
- System is running; no users exist for the test faculty.
- Valid faculty account is logged in.
Test Data:
- User ID: 1001, Name: "John Doe", Email: "john.doe@example.com"
- Faculty: "Pediatrics", Code: "PED-001"
Test Steps:
1. Faculty logs in with credentials.
2. Faculty navigates to "addUser()" operation.
3. Faculty enters: ID=1001, Name="John Doe", Email="john.doe@example.com".
4. Faculty assigns user to faculty "Pediatrics" (PED-001).
5. Faculty assigns user to camp "SummerCamp2024".
6. Faculty confirms submission.
Expected Result:
- User is created with ID=1001, name="John Doe", email="john.doe@example.com".
- User is linked to faculty "Pediatrics" and camp "SummerCamp2024".
- System logs confirmation: "User added successfully to Pediatrics/SummerCamp2024".

Title: User removal by faculty
Preconditions:
- User "John Doe" (ID=1001) exists and is assigned to faculty "Pediatrics" and camp "SummerCamp2024".
- Faculty "Pediatrics" is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Faculty logs in.
2. Faculty navigates to "removeUser()" operation.
3. Faculty searches for and selects user ID=1001.
4. Faculty confirms removal.
Expected Result:
- User "John Doe" is deleted from the system.
- User is no longer linked to faculty "Pediatrics" or camp "SummerCamp2024".
- System logs: "User ID=1001 removed successfully".

Title: Invalid user removal (non-existent user)
Preconditions:
- No user with ID=9999 exists in the system.
- Faculty is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. Faculty logs in.
2. Faculty navigates to "removeUser()" operation.
3. Faculty enters ID=9999 and attempts removal.
Expected Result:
- System displays error: "User ID=9999 not found".
- No changes to the database.
- Faculty remains on the removal page.

Title: User login/logout
Preconditions:
- User "John Doe" (ID=1001) exists and is assigned to a faculty/camp.
- System is running.
Test Data:
- Valid credentials: Email="john.doe@example.com", Password="Secure123!"
- Invalid credentials: Email="wrong@example.com", Password="wrongpass"
Test Steps:
1. User enters valid email/password and clicks "login()".
2. System verifies credentials.
3. User navigates to "logout()" and confirms.
Expected Result:
- User is authenticated and redirected to **HomeActivity**.
- User session is active; profile/graphs are accessible.
- After logout, user is redirected to **LoginActivity**.
- System logs: "User ID=1001 logged out".

Title: Invalid user login (wrong credentials)
Preconditions:
- No user exists with email="wrong@example.com".
- System is running.
Test Steps: 1. User enters invalid email/password and clicks "login()".
Expected Result:
- System displays error: "Invalid email or password".
- User remains on **LoginActivity**.
- No session is created.

Title: Home page → Monitoring section → Note → Return to home
Preconditions: - User is logged in and on the **HOME** page.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Accesso al menu a fondo" (bottom menu).
2. User selects "Sezione di monitoraggio" (monitoring section).
3. User chooses **NO** (do not monitor) in the monitoring section.
4. User is redirected to **Note** screen.
5. User clicks "Ritorno alla home" (return to home).
Expected Result:
- User navigates: **HOME → Monitoring Section → Note → HOME**.
- No data is displayed in the **Note** screen (as per "NO" condition).
- Home page reloads successfully.

Title: Home page → Monitoring section → Monitoring data → Return to home
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on the **HOME** page.
- Monitoring data exists for the user (e.g., sleep graph).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Accesso al menu a fondo".
2. User selects "Sezione di monitoraggio".
3. User chooses **SI** (monitor) in the monitoring section.
4. User views monitoring data (e.g., sleep graph).
5. User clicks "Ritorno alla home".
Expected Result:
- User navigates: **HOME → Monitoring Section → Data View → HOME**.
- Monitoring data (e.g., graph) is displayed.
- Home page reloads without data loss.

Title: Home → Profile → Graph fragments navigation
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on the **HomeActivity**.
- Profile data exists for the user.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks profile icon in **HomeActivity**.
2. User is redirected to **ProfileActivity**.
3. User clicks "GraphSonoFragment" (sleep graph).
4. User navigates back to **HomeActivity**.
5. User clicks "GraphAlattamentoFragment" (feeding graph).
6. User navigates back to **HomeActivity**.
7. User clicks "GraphCrescitaFragment" (growth graph).
Expected Result:
- Navigation flow: **HomeActivity → ProfileActivity → GraphSonoFragment → HomeActivity → GraphAlattamentoFragment → HomeActivity → GraphCrescitaFragment**.
- Each graph fragment loads data correctly (e.g., sleep/feeding/growth metrics).
- No crashes or data corruption during transitions.

Title: Create family activity record
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and on **HomeActivity**.
- User has profile data.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to **FamilyActivity** via home menu.
2. User enters:
   - Name: "Smith"
   - Surname: "Family"
   - Gender: "Male"
   - Date: "2024-05-20"
3. User clicks "createFamilyActivity".
4. System generates a random ID (e.g., 2001).
Expected Result:
- Family activity record is created with:
  - Name="Smith", Surname="Family", Gender="Male", Date="2024-05-20", ID=2001.
- System logs: "Family activity created successfully".
- Record is visible in **ProfileActivity**.

Title: Invalid family activity creation (missing required field)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to **FamilyActivity**.
2. User leaves **Name** field empty and submits.
Expected Result:
- System displays error: "Name is required".
- No record is created.
- User remains on **FamilyActivity** screen.

Title: View sleep graph data
Preconditions:
- User is logged in and has sleep data recorded.
- User is on **ProfileActivity**.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "GraphSonoFragment" (sleep graph).
2. User verifies graph displays:
   - X-axis: Time (e.g., hours).
   - Y-axis: Sleep duration (e.g., minutes).
   - Data points for past 7 days.
Expected Result:
- Graph renders correctly with labeled axes and data points.
- No errors or missing data.
- User can zoom/pan (if supported).

Title: View growth graph with no data
Preconditions:
- User is logged in but has no growth data recorded.
- User is on **ProfileActivity**.
Expected Result:
- Graph displays a message: "No growth data available".
- No errors or crashes.
- User can return to **ProfileActivity** without issues.

Title: User assigned to multiple camps (invalid scenario)
Preconditions:
- User "John Doe" (ID=1001) exists and is assigned to camp "SummerCamp2024".
- Faculty is logged in.
Test Steps: 1. Faculty attempts to assign user ID=1001 to a second camp "WinterCamp2024".
Expected Result:
- System displays error: "User already assigned to SummerCamp2024".
- User remains assigned only to "SummerCamp2024".
- No duplicate assignments are created.

Title: Faculty manages 0 camps (edge case)
Preconditions:
- Faculty "Pediatrics" exists but manages no camps.
- User "John Doe" exists but is not assigned to any camp.
Test Steps:
1. Faculty logs in.
2. Faculty attempts to assign user ID=1001 to a camp.
Expected Result:
- System displays error: "No camps available for assignment".
- User remains unassigned to any camp.
- Faculty can create a camp first (if UI allows).

Title: User logout during graph navigation
Expected Result:
- User is logged out immediately.
- System redirects to **LoginActivity**.
- No unsaved data is lost (graphs are read-only).
- System logs: "User logged out during graph view".
